modules.title: Design features and construction of Foundations
topics: Introduction and essential requirements of foundations
Types of footings and footing depth (strip footings, isolated footings, eccentrically loaded footings, grillage foundations)
Design features and construction details of combined footing and strap footing
Frost heave: causes and prevention
Effect of ground water on footings
Purpose and classification of pile foundations
Advantages, disadvantages and selection criteria for pile types
Pile action and behavior of single piles and pile groups under load
Definition of load failure for piles

modules.title: Formwork and Temporary structures
topics: Design and construction features of temporary structures
Stationary and slip formwork techniques
Special features of in‑situ construction
Stripping and removal of formworks
Formworks for special structures (e.g., shells, bridges, towers)

modules.title: Masonry and walls
topics: Brick masonry: bonds, jointing, construction methods
Stone masonry: casting and laying techniques
Masonry construction practices and code provisions for load‑bearing and non‑load‑bearing walls
Common defects in masonry walls and their impact on strength and performance
Design of brick masonry, precast stone masonry, hollow concrete block, and hollow block masonry walls
Plastering, pointing, white and colour washing, distempering, and dampness protection
Doors, windows and ventilators: classification by material, size, location, fittings
Construction of sunshades, sills, jambs, and RCC door/window frames
Stair types and rules of proportionality
Repair techniques for masonry, walls, doors and windows

modules.title: Construction of Floors and Roofs
topics: Ground floor components, material selection and construction of various floorings (mud, brick, cement, terrazzo, mosaic, tiled, marble, rubber, glass, plastic)
Upper floor: slab floors, jack‑arch floors, RCC floors, ribbed/hollow tiled flooring, filler‑joist floors, pre‑cast concrete floors, timber floors
Repair techniques for floors
Introduction to roof types and construction of pitched roofs, single roofs, double/purlin roofs, trussed roofs, steel roof trusses
Roof coverings for pitched and flat/terraced roofs
Repair techniques for roofs

modules.title: Construction of Earthquake Resistant Building
topics: Planning of earthquake‑resistant buildings
Construction of walls with corner reinforcement provisions
Construction of beams and columns for seismic performance
Base isolation techniques

experiments: Tests on Bricks
Tests on Aggregates (fine and Coarse)
Tests on Cements and concrete
Tests on tiles
reference_books: Mohan Rai & M. P. Jai Singh, Advances in Building materials and Constructions.
S. P. Arora and S. P. Bindra, A text Book of Building Construction - Dhanpat Rai and Sons, New Delhi.
S. K. Sarkar and Saraswati, Construction Technology - Oxford University Press, New Delhi.
Sushil Kumar, Building Construction.
B. C. Punmia, Building Construction.
Metchell, Building Construction.
Chudley R., Construction Technology.
Dr. K.R. Arora, Soil Mechanics & Foundation Engg - Std. Publishers Delhi.
B.C. Punmia, Soil Mechanics & Foundation Engg. - Laxmi Publications Delhi

modules.title: Virtual work and Energy Principles
topics: Principles of Virtual work applied to deformable bodies
Strain energy and complementary energy
Energy theorems
Maxwell’s Reciprocal theorem
Analysis of Pin‑Jointed frames for static loads

modules.title: Indeterminate Structures‑I
topics: Static and Kinematics indeterminacy
Analysis of Fixed and Continuous beams by theorem of three moments
Effect of sinking and rotation of supports
Moment distribution method (without sway)

modules.title: Indeterminate Structures‑II
topics: Analysis of beams and frames by slope‑deflection method
Column Analogy method

modules.title: Arches and Suspension Cables
topics: Three hinged arches of different shapes
Eddy’s Theorem
Suspension cable and stiffening girders
Two hinged and fixed arches – rib shortening and temperature effects

modules.title: Rolling loads and Influence Lines
topics: Maximum shear force and bending moment curves for various types of rolling loads
Focal length and equivalent uniform distributed load (EUDL)
Influence lines for determinate structures – beams
Influence lines for three‑hinged arches

experiments: To verify Maxwell‑Bett’s Law.
To determine the flexural rigidity of the beam and verify it theoretically.
To determine the deflection of a pin‑jointed truss and verify the results theoretically and graphically.
To verify strain in an externally loaded beam with a strain gauge indicator and verify theoretically.
To study behaviour of different types of columns and find Euler’s buckling load for each case.
To study two hinged arch for the horizontal displacement of the roller end for a given system of loading and compare with analytical results.
To study the behaviour of a portal frame under different end conditions.
To find the value of flexural rigidity (EI) for a given beam and compare it with theoretical value.
To determine the deflection of a pin‑connected truss analytically and graphically and verify experimentally.
To verify the Muller‑Breslau theorem by using Begg’s deformator set.
reference_books: Rammamurthy, Theory of Structures, Dhanpat Rai.
Bhavikatti S.S., Analysis of Structures (I & II), Vikas Publication.
B. C. Punmia, Theory of Structures, Firewall Media.
A. Kassimali, Structural Analysis, Cengage Learning.
A. Ghali, A. Neville, T. G. Brown, Structural Analysis, CRC Press.
Hibbler, Structure Analysis –1, Pearson Education India.
C. S. Reddy, Basic Structural Analysis, Tata McGraw Hill Publishing Company.
Pandit and Gupta, Theory of Structures – I, McGraw Hill.
West H. H., Fundamental of Structural Analysis, Wiley India.
Das M. M., Structural Analysis, PHI.
Thandavamurthy T. S., Structural Analysis, Oxford.
Muthuku, Azmi I., Basic Structural Analysis, IK International Publisher.
C. K. Wang, Intermediate Structural Analysis, McGraw Hill.
J. Kinney Sterling, Indeterminate Structural Analysis, Addison‑Wesley.
R. R. Mamuther, Theory of Structures, Dhanpat Rai.
Jain O. P., Jain B. K., Theory & Analysis of Structures (I & II), Nem Chand.

modules.title: Introduction, Tractive resistances \& Permanent way
topics: Principles of transportation and modes (roads, railways, airways, waterways) and their limitations.
Route surveys, alignment, railway track development and gauge considerations.
Hauling capacity and tractive effort.
Rail types, welding, wear, creep.
Sleeper types, comparison, density requirements.
Rail fastenings: fish plates, fish bolts, spikes, bearing plates, chain keys, check and guard rails.
Ballast requirements, material types, quantity, plate laying methods, material trains and relaying of track.

modules.title: Geometric Design ; Station \& Yards; Points and Crossings \& Signaling and interlocking
topics: Formation and cross‑section design, super‑elevation, equilibrium, cant and cant deficiency.
Curve geometry and permissible speeds on curves.
Types and locations of stations and yards, general equipment and layout planning.
Design of simple turnouts and layout details.
Signal types used in stations and yards and principles of signaling and interlocking.

modules.title: Bridge Site Investigation and Planning ; Loading Standards \& Component parts
topics: Site selection, alignment and collection of bridge design data.
Essential surveys, hydraulic design and scour depth assessment.
Economic span, clearance, afflux, classification of road and railway bridges.
Design loads, impact factors and Indian loading standards for railway and highway bridges.
Superstructure and sub‑structure components: abutments, piers, wing walls, return walls, approaches, decks and flooring systems.
Selection of appropriate superstructure type.

modules.title: Bridge Foundations, Construction, Testing and Strengthening of Bridges
topics: Foundation types for bridges: piles, wells, sinking wells, cofferdams.
Material selection for bridges and construction techniques (underwater and above water).
Use of sheet piles and cofferdams, erection of girders, equipment and plant.
Inspection, data collection and strengthening methods for bridges.
Causes and analysis of bridge failures.

modules.title: Tunnels
topics: Route selection, engineering surveys, alignment, tunnel cross‑section, bridge action and pressure‑relief phenomena.
Tunnel approaches, shafts and pilot shafts.
Construction methods in soft soil, hard soil and rock.
Lining types and installation methods.
Mucking, drainage and ventilation practices.
Notable examples of important tunnels in India and abroad.

experiments: Collection of photographs showing various bridge types, rail tracks, and tunnels.
Hydraulic design of bridges.
Study of modern large‑span bridges, including prestressed bridges and launching processes.
Field visit to railway bridges for rehabilitation assessment.
Field visit to railway over‑bridges and under‑bridges.
reference_books: Chakraborty and Das; Principles of transportation engineering; PHI
Rangwala SC; Railway Engineering; Charotar Publication House, Anand
Rangwala SC; Bridge Engineering; Charotar Publication House, Anand
Ponnuswamy; Bridge Engineering; TMH
Railway Engineering by Arora & Saxena - Dhanpat Rai & Sons
Railway Track by K.F. Antia
Principles and Practice of Bridge Engineering S.P. Bindra - Dhanpat Rai & Sons
Bridge Engineering - J.S. Alagia - Charotar Publication House, Anand
Railway, Bridges & Tunnels by Dr. S.C. Saxena
Harbour, Docks & Tunnel Engineering - R. Srinivasan
Essentials of Bridge Engg. By I.J. Victor; Relevant IS & IRS codes

modules.title: Introduction and physical geology
topics: branches application and scope of geology
age and parts of the earth
weathering or rocks
geological action of river
ground water
sea and oceans
Concept and causes of earthquakes and volcanoes

modules.title: Mineralogy and crystallography
topics: fundamentals of mineralogy
physical properties
study of common rock forming minerals and ore minerals
importance to civil engineering
element of crystals and introduction to crystal systems

modules.title: Petrology
topics: rock cycle
composition
classification and structures of igneous, sedimentary and metamorphic rocks of civil engineering importance
study of common rock types
brief geological history of India

modules.title: Structural geology
topics: dip, strike, outcrops
classification and detailed studies of geological structures i.e. Folds, Faults, Joints, Unconformity
their importance in civil engineering

modules.title: Applied geology and remote sensing
topics: engineering properties of rocks
selection of sites for Dam, Tunnel, Reservoirs and Canals
uses of remote sensing technique
Types, components and elements of remote sensing
EMS and MSS
Visual interpretation technique
application of GIS in civil engineering and resource mapping (site selection, water resources, rocks and soil)

experiments: Identification of simple rock forming minerals and important ores.
Identification of rocks
Simple map Exercises.
Field Visit/Geological Excursion
reference_books: Prabin Singh - "Engineering and General Geology"
P. K. Mukherjee - "A test Book of Geology"
S. K. Garg - "A text Book of Physical and Engineering Geology"

experiments: Introduction to CAD, Introduction to AutoCAD, Software and hardware requirements, various input and output devices. Getting started with AutoCAD, Setting drawing limits, Units etc.
Learning and practice of Draw commands, Modify commands, utility and other commands.
Drawing basic Geometric Shapes, Basic Plotting and Editing Tools, Architectural Views & Drafting Views.
3D modelling with AutoCAD
Dimensioning, Annotating in AutoCAD with Text & Hatching, Blocks, drafting symbols and Attributes, Layers, Templates & Design Center, Advanced plotting (Layouts, Viewports)
Drawing plan, section and elevation of 1 BHK house.
